adds.rationale: Constellation Energy's September 20, 2024 twenty-year 835 MW PPA with Microsoft to restart Three Mile Island Unit 1 is the first hyperscaler-anchored nuclear restart in US history and directly embodies the profile's 'utility PPAs to AI data centers' nuclear sub-thesis; CEG adds operating nuclear-utility cash-flow exposure distinct from URA's upstream uranium-mining exposure.

news_evidence.summary: Constellation to restart Three Mile Island Unit 1 after Microsoft signs a 20-year 835 MW PPA for AI data centers in PJM; CEG +~8% premarket; deal lifts EPS growth from 10% to 13% through 2030.
Constellation plans a 2028 restart (renamed Crane Clean Energy Center); $1.6B investment eligible for the 45Y clean-energy credit; fixed-price Microsoft contract underpins economics.
Three Mile Island to return — first US nuclear restart after full closure; Microsoft signs a 20-year 835 MW carbon-free deal for AI data-center load.

removes.rationale: Judge Mehta's August 5, 2024 ruling declared Google's core search business an illegally maintained monopoly and opened an active DOJ remedies process threatening Chrome/Android divestiture — a material legal overhang on Alphabet's primary revenue driver; AI remains covered by MSFT and NVDA, making GOOGL the weakest of the three AI names by thesis-risk-adjusted merit.

news_evidence.summary: DOJ prevails: Judge Mehta rules Google illegally monopolized general search and search text advertising via exclusive distribution contracts.
CRS analysis: court held Google maintained its monopoly unlawfully; remedies range from data-licensing/contract restrictions to structural divestiture.

rationale_overall: One swap — CEG in, GOOGL out — reduces AI concentration from three names to two while capturing the most significant nuclear catalyst of 2024: the September 20 Three Mile Island restart PPA between Constellation and Microsoft, the first hyperscaler-anchored revival of a shuttered US nuclear plant — exactly the 'utility PPA to AI data centers' sub-thesis the profile names. GOOGL's removal is supported by Judge Mehta's August 5 monopoly ruling opening a live DOJ remedies process with potential Chrome/Android divestiture; AI remains covered by MSFT (the TMI PPA counterparty, reinforcing its nuclear/AI crossover) and NVDA. Post-change watchlist: two each in AI and nuclear, one each in rockets, quantum, defense, robotics; aging-demographics remains the one uncovered gap for a future quarter.
